Class EventPerformanceController

java.lang.Object
controller.Controller
controller.EventPerformanceController
public class EventPerformanceController extends Controller
Controller responsible for event and performance-related operations in the events app.

Handles creating events with performances, searching for performances, viewing individual performance details, cancelling performances (EP only). Interacts with performances (admin only). Maintains the global collections of events and performances.

  • Constructor Details

    • EventPerformanceController

      public EventPerformanceController(View view, List<Performance> performances, external.PaymentSystem paymentSystem)
      Constructs a new EventPerformanceController.
      Parameters:
      view - the view for user interaction
      performances - the shared performance list
      paymentSystem - the shared payment system

  • Method Details

    • loadPreregisteredData

      public void loadPreregisteredData(EntertainmentProvider ep)

    • createEvent

      public Event createEvent()
      Only an EntertainmentProvider may create events. The EP is prompted for the event title, type, whether it is ticketed, and then for one or more performance details (venue, dates, ticket price, total tickets).

    • searchForPerformances

      public void searchForPerformances()
      Handles the search for performances use case.

      Prompts the user for a date, then displays all active performances on that date. If the user is a student with preferences set, matching performances are shown first (R7/R8a).

    • viewPerformance

      public void viewPerformance()
      Prompts the user for a performance ID and displays its full details. Any user can view a performance.

    • cancelPerformance

      public void cancelPerformance()
      Only the EntertainmentProvider who created the performance may cancel it. The EP is prompted for the performance ID and a cancellation message for affected students. All active bookings are refunded through the payment system and marked as cancelled by provider.
